# рҹҡҖ Complete System Design Guide - From Zero to Hero

> **Master system design interviews and build scalable systems like FAANG engineers**

## How to Use This Guide

- Read one section at a time and summarize key trade-offs in your own words.
- For each case study, follow this order: requirements -> estimation -> HLD -> deep dive -> bottlenecks.
- Time-box interview practice to 45 minutes and keep 5 minutes for wrap-up trade-offs.
- Maintain a design journal with one architecture diagram per day.

## рҹ“ҡ Table of Contents

1. [System Design Fundamentals](#1-system-design-fundamentals)
2. [Core Components](#2-core-components)
3. [Design Patterns & Architectures](#3-design-patterns--architectures)
4. [Real-World System Designs](#4-real-world-system-designs)
5. [Advanced Topics](#5-advanced-topics)
6. [Interview Preparation](#6-interview-preparation)

---

## 1. System Design Fundamentals

**Section goal:** Build a strong foundation in reliability, scale, consistency, and data flow trade-offs.

### 1.1 Key Concepts

#### Scalability
**Definition:** A system's ability to handle growth in users, data, and traffic without service degradation.

```
Vertical Scaling (Scale Up):          Horizontal Scaling (Scale Out):
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                      в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ             в”Ӯ                      в”Ӯ S1 в”Ӯ в”Ӯ S2 в”Ӯ в”Ӯ S3 в”Ӯ в”Ӯ S4 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   16 CPU    в”Ӯ                      в”Ӯ2CPUв”Ӯ в”Ӯ2CPUв”Ӯ в”Ӯ2CPUв”Ӯ в”Ӯ2CPUв”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   64GB RAM  в”Ӯ                      в”Ӯ4GB в”Ӯ в”Ӯ4GB в”Ӯ в”Ӯ4GB в”Ӯ в”Ӯ4GB в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   2TB SSD   в”Ӯ                      в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
в”Ӯ             в”Ӯ                      
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                      вҶҗ Load Balancer distributes вҶ’
                                     вң… More reliable, no single point
вқҢ Limited ceiling                   вң… Linear cost growth
вқҢ Single point of failure           вң… Better fault tolerance
вқҢ Expensive at scale                
```

**Trade-offs:**
- **Vertical scaling:** Simpler to start, but limited by machine size and single-node risk.
- **Horizontal scaling:** Better long-term elasticity and resilience, but adds distributed-system complexity.

#### Availability
**Definition:** Percentage of time system is operational.

```
Availability Levels (SLA):

99%       вҶ’ 3.65 days downtime/year     (Consumer apps)
99.9%     вҶ’ 8.76 hours downtime/year    (Business apps)
99.99%    вҶ’ 52.56 minutes downtime/year (Critical systems)
99.999%   вҶ’ 5.26 minutes downtime/year  (Financial, healthcare)
99.9999%  вҶ’ 31.5 seconds downtime/year  (Emergency services)

Formula: Availability = Uptime / (Uptime + Downtime) Г— 100%
```

#### CAP Theorem
**Definition:** In the presence of network partitions, a distributed system can prioritize either strong consistency or high availability.

```
         Consistency (C)
              в–І
              в”Ӯ
              в”Ӯ
              в”Ӯ
 Partition в—„в”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв–ә Availability
Tolerance (P) в”Ӯ       (A)

Pick 2:
в”ңв”Җ CA: Single-node or tightly-coupled systems (not partition tolerant at distributed scale)
в”ңв”Җ CP: Prioritize correctness (e.g., critical state systems)
в””в”Җ AP: Prioritize uptime and graceful degradation (eventual consistency)

In distributed systems, P is required вҶ’ Choose between C and A
```

```
   в”ӮServer 1в”Ӯ    в”ӮServer 2в”Ӯ    в”ӮServer 3в”Ӯ
   в”ӮPort:80 в”Ӯ    в”ӮPort:80 в”Ӯ    в”ӮPort:80 в”Ӯ
   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

Algorithms:
в”ңв”Җ Round Robin: Distribute evenly (S1вҶ’S2вҶ’S3вҶ’S1...)
в”ңв”Җ Least Connections: Route to server with fewest active connections

в”ңв”Җ Weighted: More powerful servers get more traffic
в””в”Җ Least Response Time: Route to fastest server
```

```python
    balance roundrobin
    option httpchk GET /health

    server web1 192.168.1.10:80 check inter 2s
```

### 1.3 Caching Strategies

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ Client в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ  CDN   в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”ӮApp Serverв”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ Database в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                   в–І               в–І
                   в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ
              Edge Cache      Application
              (Static)         Cache (Redis)

Cache Hierarchy:
1. Browser Cache (100ms)
2. CDN Cache (10-50ms)
3. Application Cache (1-5ms)
4. Database Cache (5-10ms)
```

**Cache Invalidation Strategies:**

```
1. Time-to-Live (TTL):
   SET user:123 "{data}" EX 3600  # Expires in 1 hour
   
2. Write-Through:
   Write вҶ’ Cache вҶ’ Database (both updated synchronously)
   
3. Write-Back:
   Write вҶ’ Cache вҶ’ Database (async)
   
4. Write-Around:
   Write вҶ’ Database (cache updated on read miss)

5. Cache-Aside (Lazy Loading):
   Read вҶ’ Check Cache вҶ’ If miss, load from DB вҶ’ Update cache
```

**Requirements:**

### 6.2 Common Mistakes to Avoid

вқҢ **Don't:**
- Jump into implementation
- Assume requirements
- Ignore scale/numbers
- Forget about failures
- Overcomplicate initially
- Skip trade-off discussion

вң… **Do:**
- Clarify requirements
- Start simple, iterate
- Consider trade-offs
- Think about edge cases
- Communicate clearly
- Call out assumptions explicitly
